channel.title: Frontion - Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Tier 1 geopolitical news headlines curated from Reuters, AP, BBC, AFP, Al Jazeera, Financial Times, and other trusted sources.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Sanctions Chinese Firms for Aiding Iran Drone Program
item.description: The US Treasury sanctioned 10 individuals and companies from China and Hong Kong for supplying materials for Iran's Shahed drones and ballistic missiles, escalating pressure on Tehran's weapons supply chain.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚓ CIA Report: Iran Could Withstand Hormuz Blockade for Four Months
item.description: A US intelligence analysis concludes Tehran has stockpiles to survive a naval blockade for months, as fighting flares in the Gulf despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇳 US Revises UN Iran Resolution, China and Russia Still Expected to Veto
item.description: Washington revised its proposed UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say the changes are unlikely to prevent Chinese and Russian vetoes.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Approves $25 Billion Defense Boost, Less Than Government Sought
item.description: Taiwan's opposition-controlled parliament approved extra defense spending of $25 billion, about two-thirds of what the government requested to counter China's military modernization.

item.title: 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿 Pro-Independence Parties Surge Across Britain in Historic Election Shift
item.description: Three of the United Kingdom's four nations are now set to be governed by pro-independence parties after elections that nationalists say mark a turning point for the centuries-old union.

item.title: 🏛️ Ex-NATO Chief Warns of Alliance Disintegration
item.description: Former NATO Secretary General Anders Fogh Rasmussen warned of potential alliance disintegration as European countries increasingly take leadership roles while the US role recedes under Trump administration policies.

item.title: 🛢️⚔️ US Strikes Two Iranian-Flagged Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US Central Command confirmed strikes on two empty Iranian oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz as tensions escalate despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations. The incident marks another challenge to the fragile truce.

item.title: 🇮🇶⚔️ Foreign Militias Deployed in Iran to Enforce Regime Crackdown
item.description: Iraqi Hashd al-Shaabi militias are now patrolling Iranian streets as the regime intensifies its internal crackdown, with residents saying it feels like an occupation.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iran Questions US Commitment to Diplomacy After Naval Escalation
item.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi told his Turkish counterpart that US naval escalation in the Strait of Hormuz raises doubts about Washingtons commitment to ongoing peace talks.

item.title: 🇭🇹⚖️ Four Convicted in US Trial Over Haiti Presidential Assassination
item.description: A US jury convicted four people of conspiracy in connection with the 2021 assassination of Haitian President Jovenel Moïse, with prosecutors saying the conspirators hoped to enrich themselves.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iran accuses US of reckless military adventure as Hormuz clashes continue
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the US of opting for military action whenever diplomacy is on the table, as both sides exchanged attacks in the Strait of Hormuz and the US enforced a naval blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: 💻 Major cyber attack disrupts thousands of universities and schools worldwide
item.description: Hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for an attack on Canvas academic software, affecting approximately 9,000 institutions globally. Students faced exam cancellations and coursework disruptions across US, Canada, Australia and Europe.

item.title: 🇿🇦 South African Opposition Demands Ramaphosa Resign After Court Ruling
item.description: Opposition leader Julius Malema called for President Cyril Ramaphosa to resign after the Constitutional Court ruled parliament violated the constitution by blocking impeachment proceedings over the Phala Phala cash theft scandal.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Sees Largest Anti-War Protests in Decades Over Defense Expansion
item.description: Thousands of protesters gathered in Tokyo and other cities against Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi moves to lift arms export restrictions and expand military role abroad, challenging Japan post-war pacifist constitution.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇨🇳 Iran says US cannot turn China against Tehran as peace talks loom
item.description: Iran ambassador to China says Beijing-Tehran ties are strong enough to withstand US pressure, as speculation grows Washington may offer trade concessions to China in exchange for pressuring Iran on peace deal.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 CIA report: Iran could withstand Hormuz blockade for four months
item.description: US intelligence assessment shows Iran has stockpiles and resilience to endure a naval blockade for months, raising questions about the effectiveness of a blockade strategy as fighting continues in the Gulf.

item.title: 🛢️🇯🇵🇨🇳 Two LNG tankers cross Hormuz en route to Japan and China
item.description: Two liquefied natural gas carriers have passed through the Strait of Hormuz heading to Japan and China, signaling possible easing of the blockade, though volumes remain small and Asian spot prices are up 60%.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan Parliament Approves Extra Defense Spending
item.description: Taiwans opposition-controlled parliament approved extra defense spending of $25 billion, about two-thirds of what the government sought, to bolster armed forces facing a rapidly modernizing Chinese military.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇹 Rubio Questions Allies Support on Iran After Italy Talks
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Italian PM Giorgia Meloni and questioned why allies including Italy were not backing Washington efforts to confront Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting strains in the US-Europe relationship over the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇳⚖️ US Revises UN Resolution on Iran But China Russia Still Expected to Veto
item.description: Washington has revised its proposed UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks and mining in the Strait of Hormuz, but the changes are unlikely to avert Chinese and Russian vetoes, diplomats said.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇨🇳 British MPs to Visit China This Month for First Time in Seven Years
item.description: A cross-party delegation of British lawmakers will visit China this month, in a sign of warming ties after Beijing agreed to lift sanctions on six MPs following Prime Minister Keir Starmers January visit.

item.title: 📉🇪🇺 European Shares Slide as Middle East Tensions Flare
item.description: European shares slipped on Friday in broad-based losses as a flare-up in the Middle East conflict hit risk sentiment at the end of a week dominated by geopolitics.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russia and Ukraine Trade Ceasefire Accuses Despite Three-Day Truce Deal
item.description: Russia and Ukraine accused each other of violating ceasefires as Moscow prepared for its Victory Day parade, while Trump announced both sides agreed to a 3-day ceasefire and prisoner swap.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚠️ Ukraine Running Short of Air Defence Missiles After Winter Campaign
item.description: Ukraine's air force warned it is running short of air defence missiles after Russia's massive winter attack campaign, as the country braces for further strikes.

item.title: 🇬🇧🗳️ UK Elections: Reform UK Surges as Labour Suffers Major Losses
item.description: Nigel Farage's hard-right Reform UK party made significant gains in England's local elections while Keir Starmer's Labour Party suffered major losses, with pro-independence parties now governing three of the UK's four nations.
